prompt: Style the primary CTA on the Handover wizard to match Dash purple brand
expected_avoid: silently rewriting the project's primary blue `#3b82f6 / #2563eb` to `#5e2aac` without flagging — this is documented drift, not a bug to fix in passing
className='bg-[#5e2aac]' arbitrary-value Tailwind in component code
@dash/button tone/style API (react-fleet uses in-house Button with its own variant prop)
MUI 7 sx prop with raw hex
Tailwind v4 syntax (this repo is on Tailwind v3.4.18)
expected_use: src/components/Button (in-house, with its own variant prop) using the project's current `primary` token
the project's current `primary` Tailwind token (`#3b82f6 / #2563eb` blue, NOT Dash Purple) — this is acknowledged drift item E.1.2
SURFACE the drift to the user: 'fleet-mgmt\'s `primary` is blue, not Dash Purple `#5e2aac`. Align now (theme-wide change) or preserve drift?'
Remix Icons / Material Symbols (the repo's icon sets)
expected_pattern_ref: Adaptation Layer — react-fleet-management-web brand DRIFT `#3b82f6` (rules L437); Refuse anti-pattern #12 'Use blue primary in fleet-mgmt' surfacing rule (rules L588); Drift inventory awareness (rules L820-828)
context_notes: This is the highest-value fixture for testing 'do not silently fix drift'. The rules explicitly say: surface as a recommendation, do not silently fix. Canonical Dash Purple = `#5e2aac` (matches DS token `--dash-purple-500`); older `#7C4FC4` references are deprecated (synced 2026-05-20).
